pages.title: About Me - Jo Stevens
pages.meta_description: Your Strong Voice for Cardiff East
headings: h2: Follow me on social media
pages.text_excerpt: About Me - Jo Stevens Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About Me Privacy Policy – Jo Stevens MP Office Standards Policy Website & Social Media Policy In Parliament News from Parliament News from Across Wales Policy Views Middle East Statements In Cardiff East Constituency News Annual Reports Keep in touch Monthly Newsletters Advice Surgeries Visiting Parliament Cost-of-Living Advice Hub More Search Search the website Search Menu About Me I’m Jo Stevens, your Labour Member of Parliament for Cardiff East. I was first elected to represent Cardiff Central in May 2015, and you honoured me with re-election in both June 2017 and December 2019. Following the most recent election on 4th July 2024, Cardiff Central was dissolved, and I’m grateful that voters in the newly established Cardiff East chose me to be their first woman MP. I live in the heart of Cardiff East. I’ve lived in all four Cardiff constituencies during the last 35 years and I’m really proud of our vibrant, modern, multicultural capital city. How you feel about living here, what issues concern you and what you want your MP to stand up for really matters to me – so I’d love to hear from you. Personal Background I have two adult sons. I was state educated in a village in Flintshire. North Wales, graduated from Manchester University in 1988 and for over 25 years, until being elected in 2015, I was a solicitor and director of Thompsons Solicitors , a UK wide law firm dedicated to representing trade union members and others injured or mistreated at work. Interests I’ve been a Labour Party member and activist for over 30 years. I’m a member of the GMB and Usdaw unions, and the Cooperative Party . I’m an internationalist, with a particular interest in the protection of human rights in Colombia through active involvement in the NGO Justice for Colombia , on behalf of Colombian trade unionists, human rights defenders and political prisoners there. In my spare time, I enjoy real ale, good books, the arts and music. I’m an avid follower of many sports, particularly cricket, football, rugby and darts. I’m a member of Glamorgan County Cricket Club , and a regular at Cardiff City FC and on the terraces at Cardiff Blues . In Parliament Parliamentary Roles and Responsibilities I am currently Labour’s Secretary of State for Wales, and serve in Keir Starmer’s Cabinet. Prior to this, I held several other roles. I served on the Digital, Culture, Media and Sport Select Committee for three years, then became Labour’s Shadow Secretary of State for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport before being appointed to serve as Shadow Secretary of State for Wales, a role I previously held between October 2016 and January 2017. I have also been a member of the Justice Select Committee, the Standards Committee, the Privileges Committee and the Business, Innovation and Skills Select Committee. Between January 2016 and October 2016, I was Shadow Justice Minister for Prisons and Probation and the Shadow Solicitor General, supporting the Shadow Attorney General in providing legal advice to the Shadow Cabinet. Key Expertise Justice – civil, criminal and international. Employment – particularly individual and collective employment rights, occupational health and safety, director responsibilities and organisational corporate governance. Women and equalities. Digital, culture, media and sport. Welsh affairs. Follow me on social media Contact details 116 Albany Road, Cardiff, UK View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Ian Bounds, on behalf of Jo Stevens, both at 116 Albany Road, Cardiff, CF24 3RU. ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Website & Social Media Policy - Jo Stevens
pages.meta_description: Your Strong Voice for Cardiff East
pages.text_excerpt: Website & Social Media Policy - Jo Stevens Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About Me Privacy Policy – Jo Stevens MP Office Standards Policy Website & Social Media Policy In Parliament News from Parliament News from Across Wales Policy Views Middle East Statements In Cardiff East Constituency News Annual Reports Keep in touch Monthly Newsletters Advice Surgeries Visiting Parliament Cost-of-Living Advice Hub More Search Search the website Search Menu Website & Social Media Policy This website and associated social media channels – Instagram , Facebook , X (formerly Twitter) , LinkedIn and TikTok – are managed by members of Jo Stevens MP’s team. They are designed to share news, photos, and resources about Cardiff East, including what your MP is doing in the constituency and Parliament – and how you can get involved. We want these online spaces to be a safe places for people – particularly constituents of Cardiff East – to share their comments and thoughts on the issues covered here. To make sure our online community is a safe and enjoyable environment for people to comment, question and engage in debate, we ask everyone to follow these community guidelines: We encourage respectful discussion between people. You don’t have to agree with what other people say or share, but please treat everybody with dignity, courtesy and respect. Comments expressing any of the following will be removed and reported by our moderators: a. Discrimination based on race, ethnicity, national origin, religion, sexuality, sex, gender, age or disability b. Advertising or promotion of any services or pages c. Unintelligible spam or comments that are not relevant to the topics raised on the page, or the same comment(s) on multiple posts d. Violence or harm towards MPs, our moderators, or anyone else on our page e. Misinformation and conspiracy theories f. Comments from fake accounts or bots that seek to hijack the comments Our moderators will decide which comments break these guidelines, and will hide and delete any comments that do, as well as block any users who do not follow the guidelines. To keep comment moderation manageable for our moderators, we will limit the public reply and comment functions on some posts. We may also report harassment and malicious communication to the police if needed. As always, if you live in Cardiff East and have an issue you would like to raise directly with your MP, please email: [email protected] , ensuring you include your full name and address as Parliamentary rules mean that this office is only able to represent constituents of Cardiff East. Thank you. ​ Contact details 116 Albany Road, Cardiff, UK View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Ian Bounds, on behalf of Jo Stevens, both at 116 Albany Road, Cardiff, CF24 3RU. ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Advice Surgeries - Jo Stevens
pages.meta_description: Your Strong Voice for Cardiff East
headings: h2: How Can I Help - Surgery Form
h2: Keep in touch on social media
pages.text_excerpt: Advice Surgeries - Jo Stevens Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About Me Privacy Policy – Jo Stevens MP Office Standards Policy Website & Social Media Policy In Parliament News from Parliament News from Across Wales Policy Views Middle East Statements In Cardiff East Constituency News Annual Reports Keep in touch Monthly Newsletters Advice Surgeries Visiting Parliament Cost-of-Living Advice Hub More Search Search the website Search Menu Advice Surgeries As well as my work in Parliament, my office and I are available to help constituents all year round, with a range of issues, including for example benefit entitlements, HMRC issues, housing, home office and passport issues. If you need any help with any issue, or require an appointment please contact us via phone ( 02921 329736 ) or email ( [email protected] ) and a member of my team will be happy to help. I hold regular Advice surgeries. These are an opportunity for any resident living in Cardiff East to come and chat with me about any issue you might need assistance with or, to ask any question you have about my work in Parliament and here, on your behalf. Please call the office on the number above to make an appointment or fill in the form below: How Can I Help - Surgery Form First Name (Required) Last Name (Required) Address (Required) Strict Parliamentary protocol states that a Member of Parliament can only act on the behalf of their own constituents and not the constituents of other MPs. If you live outside Cardiff East constituency, you can find your MP and their contact details here: http://www.parliament.uk/mps-lords-and-offices/mps Street Address Address Line 2 City Postcode Email Address Telephone Number (Required) How can I help? Consent View the privacy policy here: https://www.jostevens.co.uk/privacy-policy-jo-stevens-mp/ I agree to the Privacy Policy CAPTCHA Keep in touch on social media Contact details 116 Albany Road, Cardiff, UK View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by Ian Bounds, on behalf of Jo Stevens, both at 116 Albany Road, Cardiff, CF24 3RU. ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
